scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: The 5-second read is 'a long, repetitive list of Untitled draft studies' — the hero R6 study is the 9th plan card and is not findable amid the clutter, and the 'why' (finished plan -> recruit a firm) is not established on-screen because nothing reads as finished (every plan 'Draft', every study 'defining').
fix_recommendation: Add a status filter/sort or a 'Ready to solicit' section that floats the one finished/approved plan to the top, with a clear ready-vs-draft cue, so an outsider sees the finished->solicit path instead of an undifferentiated draft list.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: 'Create solicitation' sits as one of four equally-weighted buttons (Archive / Submit for review / Create solicitation / Delete) with no primacy next to Delete, so the one-click launch claim is not emphasized; the post-click solicitation-with-source-attached is never shown in this frame.
fix_recommendation: Promote 'Create solicitation' to a single primary (filled) button per card with Archive/Submit/Delete collapsed into an overflow menu, so the launch affordance is unambiguously the primary action.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Multiple visible issues in one frame: a column of 7+ identical 'Untitled study' cards (placeholder/fixture data signalling an unfinished build), three '0 wards / (plans not in this program)' cross-scope rows, two duplicate 'Attakar' plan cards, the logo mark overlapping the 'Microplan portfolio' H1, and a flat type hierarchy where titles/badges/counts are similarly weighted.
fix_recommendation: Suppress or collapse Untitled/0-ward/out-of-program studies (rename-on-create or hide until named), de-duplicate the Attakar cards, fix the header logo/H1 overlap, and introduce a clear type scale so study titles anchor each card. PRODUCT/seed-data fix — never crop the capture.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: The concept_claim traces to spine S3 and the 'Create solicitation' button is genuinely present on every card, but the 'from a FINISHED micro-plan' half of the claim is asserted, not evidenced — every plan reads 'Draft' and every study 'defining', so the finished-state precondition the why depends on is not visible.
fix_recommendation: Surface an approved/finished state on the R6 plan and study (e.g. a 'Reviewed'/'Ready' badge replacing 'Draft'/'defining') so the 'launch from a finished plan' provenance is demonstrated on-screen, not assumed. Alternatively soften the narration to 'a draft plan'.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: ADVISORY. The 'Create solicitation' button is genuinely present, but 'her finished study' is not demonstrated (R6 study reads 'defining', '1 wards') and the one-click result (solicitation carrying the plan as source) is off-frame, so a viewer must trust the narrator that the click finishes the path.
fix_recommendation: Capture the R6 study in a finished/approved state and show the immediate post-click state (solicitation draft with the plan pre-attached) so the narration's 'finished study -> call for a firm' is visibly demonstrated.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: No spinner/error/dead-end in the still, but the lower half is dominated by a repetitive near-empty list of Untitled/0-ward cards that reads as seed noise rather than a curated flow — a visible rough edge in the frame itself.
fix_recommendation: Trim the placeholder study cards so the portfolio shows curated, named studies; a clean list removes the 'is this half-built?' friction a viewer feels on first scroll.

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: The pre-seed is coherent (title/scope arrive filled, the coverage panel renders attached and snapshotted), but the audience's decision is 'set scope and publish' and the form as shown is not publishable: Description, all three dates, Estimated Scale, Contact Email, Application Questions, and Evaluation Criteria are empty with no visible required-field validation, so the path to 'publish' has unexplained gaps.
fix_recommendation: Mark required fields and show inline validation state so the owner sees exactly what remains before Create Solicitation is enabled; consider pre-seeding a default deadline/scale from the plan group to reinforce the 'already waiting' promise.

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: Clean, consistent card system, but the load-bearing coverage detail (2 wards / 840 work areas / study arms) is rendered in small muted-gray text that sits quieter than the empty form scaffolding above it, so the hierarchy under-weights the one panel the scene exists to sell.
fix_recommendation: Increase size/weight and contrast of the coverage card's stat line (wards / work areas / study arms) and give the card a stronger container so the attached plan reads as the focal point of the page.

scene: 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: The core interaction has a visible incoherence: 'Which areas can you cover? / Select one or more' presents exactly ONE checkbox (a degenerate choice with nothing to select between), and the 'Application Questions' card is EMPTY (header + divider, no fields), reading as a stubbed/unfinished section.
fix_recommendation: Render the Application Questions the solicitation actually defines inside that card (or replace the empty card with an explicit 'No additional questions for this solicitation' state); and when only one coverage area exists, label it honestly ('This solicitation covers one area — confirm you can staff it') rather than 'Select one or more'.

scene: 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: Functional and clean, but hierarchy is flat where it matters: the load-bearing required checkbox is the smallest, least prominent element while the secondary AI-coach card is the visual heavyweight of the page, and the empty Application Questions card is a large block of dead space mid-frame.
fix_recommendation: Elevate the coverage selector (card it, add a required asterisk/colored marker, give the checkbox more weight) so the must-do field outranks the optional AI-coach helper, and eliminate the empty-card dead space.

scene: 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: ADVISORY. The concept_claim's second clause — 'submission is blocked until one area is chosen' — is NOT demonstrated: the blocked-submit error string is absent and Submit Response is not visibly disabled in the still. Secondary mismatch: the AI-coach card claims it 'reviews your draft answers' but there are no answer fields (empty Questions card).
fix_recommendation: Capture the scene at the moment Submit is attempted with nothing ticked (showing the disabled state or the 'Select at least one coverage area to submit.' error) so the blocking claim is visibly demonstrated; ensure answer fields exist before the AI-coach card asserts it reviews them.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: The core review affordances exist and the row is readable, but the load-bearing 'Award' action is visually clipped at the viewport's right edge — a real interaction-soundness gap for the very next step the demo reaches for.
fix_recommendation: Make the responses table responsive: wrap it in an overflow-x container or pin the ACTIONS column sticky-right so View/Review/Award are never clipped at the viewport, and ensure the Award trophy renders fully.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: Content overflows the viewport — the ACTIONS header is clipped to 'A' and the Award icon is cut at the right edge (a product layout bug, max 2-3), compounded by green being overloaded across four unrelated elements (SELECTED COVERAGE eyebrow, coverage pill, Active status pill, action icons).
fix_recommendation: Fix the table layout so the ACTIONS column fits inside the viewport (responsive widths / overflow container / sticky action column), and disambiguate the green usage so coverage pills, status pills, and action icons don't all read as the same signal.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: Single still of a clean default page with no spinners or error states; the parked cursor occluding the breadcrumb mid-word and the half-clipped action column keep it at the clean-default baseline rather than above it.
fix_recommendation: Park the cursor off load-bearing text during capture, and ensure the actions column is fully in-frame (driven by the same responsive-table product fix).

scene: 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: The honesty/look-ahead beat is conveyed by the Award action being present-but-not-wired, yet Award is the exact element clipped off the right edge and the frame is byte-for-byte identical to scene 4, so a fresh viewer cannot read 'recruiting is done, live opportunity is the next step' from the screen — the 5-second read returns the wrong (no-change) message.
fix_recommendation: Make the Award action fully visible and the look-ahead legible: either re-point scene 5 to a distinct capture (Award control focused/hovered with its aspirational treatment), or add an inline 'Next step: stand up the live opportunity' affordance/tooltip on Award so the beat is carried by pixels, not the scene title. Options — surface to user.

scene: 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: The review table is a sound surface and the honest deferral of provisioning is correct (not penalized), but reusing a frame identical to scene 4 for a distinct narrative beat is a real narrative-distinctness weakness — the viewer must be told, not shown, that anything advanced.
fix_recommendation: Give the last beat a distinct on-screen state from scene 4 — surface the Award control prominently with an aspirational/'coming soon' treatment, or add a small forward-looking panel — so the 'next step' beat reads as a deliberate progression. Options — surface to user.

scene: 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: Content overflow/occlusion: the ACTIONS header is clipped to 'A' and the Award trophy/label runs off the right viewport edge — and it is the load-bearing control of this beat — plus the cursor occludes the breadcrumb text.
fix_recommendation: Fix the responses-table layout so the ACTIONS column (View / Review / Award) is fully within the viewport at the capture width (reduce column widths, allow wrapping, or set a max content width), and position the cursor off the breadcrumb during capture.

scene: 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: ADVISORY. The scene's forward-looking 'standing up the real opportunity' narration references the Award action as the next-step cue, but that element is clipped off-frame and no provisioning UI is shown — narration references a UI element not legibly present in the still. Honest deferral of provisioning (G1) is correct and not penalized.
fix_recommendation: Ensure the Award control the narration leans on is visible in-frame so the still corroborates the look-ahead claim; honest deferral of provisioning stays as-is.

scene: 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: Because scene 5 is identical to scene 4, the viewer experiences a 'nothing new happened' beat and must conceptually re-derive the progression themselves — the scene order forces a static/backwards conceptual step rather than advancing.
fix_recommendation: Differentiate scene 5's captured state from scene 4 (a distinct Award-focused or look-ahead affordance) so the sequence reads as forward motion into the 'next step', not a repeated frame. Options — surface to user.
